tldr: Stark embeds accessibility checks inside design tools like Figma, while Accessibility Insights is a free Microsoft-maintained guided WCAG checker for web and Windows. Switching means moving from design-time plugin checks to runtime guided audits performed by developers and QA.
why_switch: Need WCAG checks on shipped pages, not just designs
Guided WCAG assessments cover web and Windows
Bring accessibility checks closer to engineering
what_to_export: Contrast and focus-order findings per design
Tickets filed from Stark into your tracker
export_method: Stark's findings live in your Figma files and Stark workspace. Open each design file and capture the annotations, contrast results and focus-order notes; document them in your shared drive or design system. Use any export options the Stark dashboard offers to save audit summaries. Designs themselves stay in Figma. Tickets filed elsewhere remain in your tracker. Treat the exported summaries plus the design files as the migration baseline.
import_method: Accessibility Insights does not require a data import; it is a free local browser extension and Windows tool. Install it on every developer and QA machine. Re-run FastPass and Assessment on the same pages, components and flows you previously analysed in Stark. Use the in-app export to save Assessment HTML or JSON per page, and file issues directly into GitHub or Azure DevOps from the tool. Use your archived Stark findings as a checklist to confirm the same issues are caught at runtime.
gotchas: Accessibility Insights is runtime-focused; you lose Stark's design-time coverage if designers stop using a plugin
No central dashboard or multi-site monitoring
Manual effort is needed for full Assessment coverage
FastPass is quick and not a persisted database; archiving exports is your responsibility
Consider keeping minimal Stark seats for designers

email.subject: Switching from Stark to Accessibility Insights
email.body:
Hi team,

Over the next 14 days we will be moving our default accessibility checks from Stark to Accessibility Insights. Stark has been great for designers in Figma, but we need stronger runtime WCAG coverage on shipped pages, and Accessibility Insights is a free Microsoft-maintained tool that gives us that.

Plan:
- Days 1-3: archive Stark annotations and audit summaries from key Figma files.
- Days 4-7: install Accessibility Insights for developers and QA and run FastPass on top flows.
- Days 8-12: standardize on Assessment-mode runs for major releases and export reports.
- Days 13-14: finish the cutover and reduce Stark seats to design-only usage.

We may keep a small number of Stark seats so designers can still catch issues early in Figma.

Thanks,
Your customer
